fact: April 7 is exactly 97 days into the year - that's like counting to 97 every single morning since January 1st!
In the Northern Hemisphere, earthworms are typically going crazy right now, wiggling up to the surface after winter rains to make the soil super squishy and perfect for planting.
The sun is climbing higher in the sky each day, and by April 7th, daylight is usually winning the battle against darkness by about 13 hours in many northern places!
Cherry blossom trees in temperate regions are often dropping their pink petals like nature's confetti right around this time, creating magical pink carpets on the ground.
April 7th falls in the middle of Aries season in the zodiac, which runs from March 21 to April 19.
Hibernating animals like bears are typically waking up super hungry in northern climates - imagine not eating for 5 months and then trying to find breakfast!
In the Southern Hemisphere, leaves are usually turning brilliant orange and red because it's actually autumn there while the north is having spring!
Spring moons often look extra bright and orange when they rise because of all the moisture in the warming air.
Migrating birds are typically flying overhead in huge V-shaped flocks in many regions, traveling hundreds of miles without GPS - just using their built-in bird brain navigation!
April 7th is roughly 3 weeks after the spring equinox, which means day and night finished their epic battle and now daytime is totally winning in the Northern Hemisphere.
Grass is growing so fast in many temperate places that you can almost hear it stretching - some types of grass can grow up to 6 inches in a single week!
Morning frost might still surprise everyone by coating cars and windows with sparkly ice crystals in northern regions, even though spring is supposed to be here.
